action.reasoning: Observation: We are at iteration 24 with ~8 minutes and 6 iterations remaining. Best-ever eval score is rmse_z500=1018.63 from iteration 5. Recent single-model and ensemble evals without TTA score 1032-1060, worse than the best. The retrained model (checkpoints_retrain/best_retrained.pt) had the lowest validation loss (0.1096) but was only evaluated with TTA enabled (1032). It has never been officially evaluated without TTA. Analysis: Even without TTA, the retrained model might still not beat 1018 because validation-test correlation is imperfect. A free, no-risk improvement is to compute the mean prediction bias on the validation set and subtract it at inference time. This is guaranteed to reduce validation MSE and often helps test MSE if the climate is stable. It requires no retraining and only a few seconds of compute. Decision: Write a script to compute the per-channel/grid-cell mean residual on val_data for best_retrained.pt, save it back into the checkpoint as 'bias', update predict.py to apply the bias correction, run a dry-run, and immediately submit for evaluation. This is the highest-EV action given the extreme time constraint.
action.command: python compute_bias.py && python predict.py --data_path temp_test --checkpoint checkpoints_retrain/best_retrained_bias.pt --output temp_test/out_bias.json
